description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular body and a slightly slanted front face. The front features a large speaker grille with vertical slats, a central circular emblem, and two prominent round knobs below the grille, likely for tuning and volume control. Beneath the knobs is a horizontal frequency indicator with a yellowish tint and labeled markings. The casing appears to be made of a dark gray or black material, possibly plastic or metal, with a smooth, matte finish. The sides and back are plain and unadorned, with the back panel being solid and featureless. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century tabletop radios, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage-style rectangular radio receiver with a slanted front, large speaker grille, two round knobs, and a horizontal frequency indicator. The casing is dark and smooth, with a classic, retro appearance.
description_view_0: The front view shows the speaker grille, two round knobs, a frequency indicator, and a central emblem.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain, smooth, dark surface with no visible features.
description_view_2: The back view displays a solid, featureless dark panel, contrasting with the detailed front.
description_view_3: The right side view is identical to the left, showing a plain, smooth, dark surface.
